acceptance.criterion: The static guide takes a user from installation through a complete run, worker visibility, review, and optional merge with accurate, equivalent Claude Code and Codex instructions.
The guide is responsive, keyboard usable, WCAG AA conscious, dependency-free, and free of promotional copy, em dashes, emojis, and the named humanizer warning patterns.
A same-repository GitHub Actions workflow publishes only `guide/` to GitHub Pages, and the deployed guide returns a successful response after merge.
README links to the live guide, the changelog explicitly records the guide and Pages site, and the end-to-end and durable docs agree with the native-first current flow.
Gemini 3.5 performs a critical clarity review, material findings are resolved, focused checks and repository verification pass, and the reviewed PR is landed by regular merge commit.

continuation_guard.next_required_action: Create the guide and Pages workflow, then align the public docs.
continuation_guard.reason: The single documentation batch and authorized landing work remain.

master_acceptance.criterion: A user can open `https://aigorahub.github.io/elves/` and follow one complete, current Elves workflow without reading internal contracts first.
The guide, README, changelog, host references, and durable docs use accurate Claude Code and Codex invocation wording and preserve the same authority model.
The documentation PR is reviewed and merged with a regular merge commit, v2.5.0 is published from that merge, and both global installs on this machine match it.
